title: ResourceEntry
description: A single safety resource or service organization entry in the Access to Safety directory. Each entry represents one organization or program that a survivor may contact for help.

name.description: Full legal or commonly known name of the organization or program.

type.description: Primary category of service this organization provides.

pod.description: The Safety Pod(s) this resource primarily belongs to. A resource may serve multiple pods.

podLabels.description: Reference labels for pod identifiers. Not required; provided here for documentation.

phone.description: Primary phone number. Use consistent formatting, e.g., 1-800-799-7233 or (512) 555-0100.

phoneCrisis.description: After-hours or crisis-specific phone number, if different from the primary number.

phoneText.description: Text or SMS number, if available (e.g., 'Text START to 88788').

phoneTTY.description: TTY/TDD number for Deaf and hard-of-hearing callers.

address.description: Street address. May be omitted for confidential shelter locations — use 'Confidential – call for address' in that case.

city.description: City where the organization is physically located or primarily serves.

state.description: Two-letter US state abbreviation, full state name, or province/territory.

region.description: The geographic region this directory entry belongs to. Should match the directory's region metadata. Used to scope queries.

geographicAreaServed.description: Description of the area this organization actually serves, which may differ from where it is located. E.g., 'All of Harris County', 'Statewide', 'Within 50 miles of Austin'.

hours.description: Hours of operation. Use plain text descriptions for each type of availability.

notes.description: Any notes on holidays, seasonal changes, or appointment-only access.

email.description: Public contact email, if available. Do not include personal staff email addresses.

services.description: List of specific services offered by this organization. Be as specific as possible.

languages.description: Languages in which services are provided. Include interpreter/translation services.

wheelchairAccessible.description: Whether the physical location is wheelchair accessible.

screenReaderFriendlyWebsite.description: Whether the organization's website is screen-reader accessible.

interpreterServices.description: Whether professional interpreter services (other than ASL) are available.

publicTransitAccessible.description: Whether the location is accessible by public transit.

notes.description: Any additional accessibility notes or accommodations offered.

cost.description: Cost to the survivor for services.

costNotes.description: Additional detail on cost — e.g., 'Free for DV survivors; sliding scale for others' or 'Medicaid accepted'.

eligibility.description: Who can use this service. Be specific about any restrictions (gender, age, county of residence, immigration status, etc.). If open to all, write 'All survivors'.
examples: All survivors of domestic violence or sexual assault

lastVerified.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this entry was last verified for accuracy.

verifiedBy.description: Name or identifier of the person who last verified this entry. May use 'anonymous volunteer' if privacy is a concern.

notes.description: Any additional information relevant to survivors using this resource — context, tips, warnings, referral process, etc.

active.description: Whether this organization is currently active. Set to false if the organization has closed or the program has ended; do not delete entries so the history is preserved.

confidentialLocation.description: Set to true if the physical address of this resource (e.g., a shelter) must not be published. When true, the address field should read 'Confidential – call for address'.
